adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Carefully" tells HOW Alex counted the money. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Loudly" tells HOW the door slammed. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.sentence: Remember to speak {quietly} in the library.
adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Quietly" tells HOW to speak. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Suddenly" tells HOW the music stopped. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.sentence: Mona could not see {clearly} in the fog.
adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Clearly" tells HOW Mona could see. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Soon" tells WHEN Jen will get braces.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Everywhere" tells WHERE the ladybugs are flying.

adverbs.sentence: Mr. Perkins will cut the grass {tomorrow}.
adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
options: How he will cut the grass
When he will cut the grass
Where he will cut the grass
adverbs.explain: "Tomorrow" tells WHEN Mr. Perkins will cut the grass.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Today" tells WHEN they played kickball.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Here" tells WHERE to put the forms.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Loyal" describes the noun "friend". Adjectives describe nouns, so this is an adjective.

adverbs.sentence: The story takes place in a {fancy} castle.
adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Fancy" describes the noun "castle". That makes it an adjective.

adverbs.sentence: Guards help children cross the street {safely}.
adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Safely" tells HOW children cross the street. It describes the verb "cross", so it's an adverb.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Later" tells WHEN they can swim. It describes the verb "swim", so it's an adverb.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Carefully" tells HOW to read the instructions. It's an adverb.

adverbs.sentence: {Now} we will go to the ice rink.
adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Now" tells WHEN we will go. It describes the verb "go".

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Straight" tells HOW to drive. It describes the verb "drive".

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Inside" tells WHERE the cats stay. It describes the verb "stay".

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Playfully" tells HOW the puppy pounces. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Amazing" describes the noun "swimmer". That makes it an adjective.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Patiently" tells HOW Olivia waits. It describes the verb "waits", so it's an adverb.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
adverbs.explain: "Hopefully" tells HOW Zeke looks. It describes the verb "looks", so it's an adverb.

adverbs.question: Is the highlighted word an adjective or an adverb?
options: Adverb — tells how she needs it
Adverb — tells when she needs it
adverbs.explain: "New" describes the noun "watch". That makes it an adjective.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Shyly" tells HOW Jonah smiled. It ends in -ly.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Always" tells WHEN (how often) Celia works. It's an adverb of time.

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Outside" tells WHERE Celia works. It describes the verb "works".

adverbs.question: What does this adverb tell us?
adverbs.explain: "Nearby" tells WHERE Jonah sat. It describes the verb "sat".

adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Quietly" is an adverb. It tells HOW Zina spoke. Adverbs describe verbs.
"Loudly" is an adverb. It tells HOW Raul sang.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: The principal spoke ___ to our guest.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Nicely" is an adverb. It tells HOW the principal spoke.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: The cat jumped ___ off the table.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Softly" is an adverb. It tells HOW the cat jumped.
"Excitedly" is an adverb. It tells HOW Jem clapped.
"Carefully" is an adverb. It tells HOW Pete stepped.
"Quickly" is an adverb. It tells HOW Dave finished his homework.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: Gayle waited ___ for the mail truck.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Eagerly" is an adverb. It tells HOW Gayle waited.
"Away" tells WHERE the deer ran. It's an adverb of place.
"Today" tells WHEN they went to the park. It's an adverb of time.
"Soon" tells WHEN they will be home. It's an adverb of time.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: I bent ___ to see the ants.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Down" tells WHERE (which direction) I bent. It's an adverb of place.
"Inside" tells WHERE they played. It's an adverb of place.
"Carefully" is an adverb — it tells HOW the driver parked. Use adverbs to describe verbs.
"Clever" is an adjective — it describes the noun "puppy". Use adjectives to describe nouns.
"Slowly" is an adverb — it tells HOW Tim walked. Use adverbs to describe verbs.
"Cheerfully" is an adverb — it tells HOW Peg whistled.
"Smooth" is an adjective — it describes the noun "dancer". Adverbs can't describe nouns.
"Loud" is an adjective — it describes the noun "voice".
"Joyfully" is an adverb — it tells HOW the choir sang.
"Strong" is an adjective — it describes the noun "player".
"Brightly" is an adverb — it tells HOW the spotlight shines.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: The ___ child stood behind his mom.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Shy" is an adjective — it describes the noun "child".
"Silently" is an adverb — it tells HOW the squirrel sat.
"Then" is an adverb of time — it tells WHEN they visited Yan.
"Yesterday" is an adverb of time — it tells WHEN they drove.
"Outside" is an adverb of place — it tells WHERE they stood.
"Swiftly" is an adverb — it tells HOW I ran.
"Loudly" is an adverb — it tells HOW they laughed.
"Weak" is an adjective — it describes the noun "fence".
"Weird" is an adjective — it describes the noun "sound".
"Calmly" is an adverb — it tells HOW Fred spoke.

adverbs_uk_2.sentence: We raced up and down the hilly track ___.
adverbs_uk_2.explain: "Wildly" tells HOW they raced — it adds exciting detail. "Soon" tells when, which doesn't fit here.
"Loudly" is an adverb that tells HOW I screamed. "Louder" is a comparison, not an adverb here.
